completed_steps.summary: Detected a Python backend/CLI/library style local-first AI GM game engine kernel, not a Unity/Godot-style graphical client.
Scanned top-level directories and critical rpg_engine modules, excluding runtime caches and save-like artifacts.
Documented player-safe, low-level runtime, platform sidecar, prewarm, intent, context, preview/proposal and commit boundaries.
Summarized packaged SQLite migrations, JSON schemas, Campaign Package, Save Package and runtime data boundaries.
Captured local setup, CI-aligned commands, BMAD workflow guardrails and public repository constraints.
Recorded expert review findings, corrections, validation results and next migration risks.

project_classification.repository_type: monorepo-like single Python package with docs, examples, scripts and runtime materials
project_classification.architecture_type: local-first AI GM engine kernel with CLI, MCP, runtime facade, SQLite state and AI intent orchestration

technology_stack.tech_summary: Python >=3.11, PyYAML, jsonschema, optional MCP, pytest, coverage, ruff, build and twine.

batches_completed.summary: Critical runtime, save, intent, context, platform, action, package and infrastructure modules scanned by role.
Test inventory counted and full pytest suite executed after documentation changes.
Legacy Markdown docs counted and treated as migration source material, not canonical truth.
Packaged migrations, schemas, examples and eval resources scanned for model and package boundaries.

resume_instructions: Round 1 Deep Scan is complete. Resume by using these _bmad-output artifacts as reviewed scan material to create canonical BMAD docs under docs/, then inventory and migrate legacy docs one by one.

scan_notes.legacy_docs_policy: Do not bulk-copy old docs. Treat them as sources to classify as valid, partially valid, stale or archive-only.
scan_notes.runtime_data_policy: Do not publish runtime data such as .aigm/, saves/, Save Packages, player save databases, platform session bindings or preflight cache contents.
